Pain in your gut bends you over and fluid spills
onto the deck
onto the ground
into your [pc.lowerUndergarment]
. This is it: your child is making its way down your birth canal.
\n\nYou grab the nearest medkit and head for your bed, determined to deliver the baby safely. After setting aside your gear, you lie down and begin to breathe in preparation for your labor.

\n\nGroaning at the timing, you shed your [pc.gear] and position yourself the best you can in the inhospitable and non-hospital-able terrain. The wish that you’d stayed somewhere indoors and safe hums through your thoughts like a mosquito, but there’s no help for it - you’ll have to deliver the baby on your own.

\n\nYou waddle as fast as you can to the nearest restroom. There’s a med-drone fastened to the wall, installed for this exact eventuality. It instructs you to remain calm as it positions a blanket beneath you, and to bear down when it beeps.
\n\nSpasms wrack your pregnant body for the next hour as it works the baby free. It is a tough, arduous process: Your bundle of joy is a big so-and-so, struggling and hard-boned.
You have no choice but to bear it; richly cursing the baby, yourself, and the world at large for having to bear a child in this day and age like this.
(o?"The drone administers":"You administer")+" some painkillers, and the whole thing goes on in a fuzzy blur.

\n\nAt last you hear a throaty wail; somebody announcing themselves to the universe. Exhausted, you cut the umbilical cord and draw the damp, wriggling child up into your arms. To the casual observer perhaps, it would look like you’ve given birth to a brown-skinned, particularly large human girl. A mother’s eye lands upon the long, preternaturally curved back; the pointed posture of the feet, and the keratinous heels that support them; and of course, the pointed, elven ears.

\n\nYour dzaan child is "+(0===t?"female, a so-called beta of the species":"a hermaphrodite, a rare alpha of the species")+", and once she’s stopped contorting her face into hearty screams and is contenting herself with the [pc.milk]-dripping [pc.nipple] you present to her, you can see for yourself that she is both whole and a real cutie.

\n\nYou nurse her peacefully for a while, lost in the throes of exhausted motherhood. At last her head droops against your arm, carried off into a deep sleep. You summon the drone, carefully sequester her into the climate-controlled device, and watch it bear your new child off to the nursery, where years of quiet safety await. You clean yourself up, and then disappear into a long, well-needed slumber yourself.

		Zhèng Shi is fraught with unease. The sounds of heavy construction and lurid passions reach from the gloom to nibble at your [pc.ears], and creeping between them is the threat of ambush. No one enjoys being surrounded by shadows much. Not in a veritable heart of darkness like this station.
		
		Sheer luck didn’t bring you here. Experience, training, and a little bit of wit are why you’re still alive, still wandering through a pirate base in a sector of space the U.G.C. doesn’t control.

Ever since arriving, you’ve stepped on the toes of powerful warlords. You’ve impugned their authority, disrupted their operations. Such loss of face in a dog-eat-dog universe is unacceptable. ZS is too organized, too evil, too incomprehensible — it will react. It will come after you. Deep down, you’re very aware that causing any more damage will be met with an extreme response, if it isn’t already being prepared.
You can’t wait for it.
\n\nIt happens again. Paranoia worms into your skin, crawls down your spine. Another fruitless scan of the environment doesn’t assuage it this time. Have you been singled out
yet again by some well-trained operator

textify(U1||(U1=(0,Ce.Z)([" A troupe of kaithrit play music while one dressed in long, flowing slips of orange and gold dances for a crowd. She’s a mesmerizing, engaging performer who purrs in the ears of generous benefactors: many credit chits are deposited into a prominent donation box. You watch for a while, which is the point, seeing how she flashes cleavage and adroitly evades the sneaky hands looking to cop a feel. But when her body moves, revealing the men and women opposite, your pupils shrink. It’s a fight-or-flight response. You blink, rub them, and what was there is gone. It was a figure in all black, you swear. In darkness was she walking. What should be a quaint attraction feels like a prison. Muscling your way out of the entrapment, you scan your environment. Human in a black suit... black-and-green styled punky ausar... gray and black leithan... Black laquine. No way. Again you blink. It can’t be... “Watch it!” Your sight is broken by the impolite shove of one of the universe’s innumerable dickheads, carrying himself like the promenade’s stuck-up owner. Sophora -- no, you can’t know that. The laquine you saw is gone. Maybe it’s just your mind playing tricks on you. There’s another black-furred laquine, whose ears reach for the sky. A happy woman, by your quick estimate. It doesn’t do anything for your heart. "])))

textify(q1||(q1=(0,Ce.Z)([" “Hey hello, how’re you doing?” A down-to-earth-looking guy with a glowing bronze complexion waves you into his group. Apparently, they need a third opinion on who’s telling the best joke today. Sure, you can humor them. Each one’s quite a character, gleaming with the sweat of a hard day’s job. Pretty soon you’re laughing and smiling with them as though you’d known them for your whole life. One playfully elbows you in the ribs. “So, which one do you like?” Before you can even open your mouth, you catch something over his shoulder. The mirth visibly fades from your [pc.face], something they pick up on. Everyone looks down the alley. Nothing. But... you saw something... something lapine-shaped. Something with red eyes. Maybe it’s just the heat. All’s quiet. Another claps your shoulder. “You alright? Wanna get a drink? It’s fuckin’ killer out today.” If you weren’t suddenly paranoid, you might have agreed. Polite farewells are exchanged, and here you’re standing alone among milling colonists. Your [pc.eyes] are on the shadows between buildings, unblinkingly searching. Sophora can’t be here. No way. And there’s no way she can attack you in town. You exhale, coolness injecting into your bloodstream. Maybe your microsurgeons have anti-anxiety functions. It’s welcome. Everything is going to be alright. "])))

textify(W1||(W1=(0,Ce.Z)(["
		Getting bored with Novahome is the remotest possibility. The native raskvel inhabiting it are exploding with energy twenty-four-seven, hawking items of dubious value, entertaining their guests, lending long, long ears to tall tales beyond Tarkus’s atmosphere. Smiling at the big picture comes easy: there’s always some new, convivial detail to glance at; the fervor in this shabby comfort never wanes.

		Not for them, but for you. Your mind’s eye detects that which it associates the purest kind of hair-raising anxiety with. The corridors around you mute as your pupils shrink, fight or flight response stirring. You stand idle there and for a moment strain to detect... what that was. Something in all black. Something with disturbing eyes.

		...Sophora? No... no, it was just a raskvel’s ears. Look at that pair in the distance, one raskvel perched on the shoulders of another, darker scales than usual. Shake your head. Nothing.

		You turn, decide to get scarce, and there it is again. Is it? Something rabbit-like staring at you and drifting from sight.

		Suddenly, you notice none of the excitement. Everything feels drab. Isolated. Nothing would dare strike at you here, still... still. That Jumper’s living in your head rent-free...
		"])))

textify(V1||(V1=(0,Ce.Z)(["
		Myrellion is a hot zone — a hot destination for megacorps keen on seizing the planet’s natural wealth and native genetic data, and Rushers looking to partake in the exotic experiences here before the natives presumably self-destruct the UGC’s once-in-a-lifetime chances.
		
		You sigh. [var0
			|Gold myr congregate through their city, color sergeants ululating orders in untranslated tongue. Barricades and lines are positioned to deflect unwanted visitors from Gildenmere’s inner secrets.
			|Lithe, neatly-dressed red myr officers patrol their streets in tactical units of three, catching the ever-pleased attentions of off-worlders. The gleam of venomous fangs hints and the flex of arms strongly warn against getting too handsy.
			|Red myr with no-nonsense miens and Gold myr faintly bubbling with anger and resentment tolerate each other just barely in the DMZ. Between them are unconcerned star-walkers eager to sample the delights offered by the warring states.
		] Be prepared to flee for your ship at a moment’s notice, you suppose. One explosion and both sides will probably go wild.
		
		Not that anyone in the UGC would break up inside over it. Certainly not Sophora, wandering over —
		
		You blink, rub your [pc.eyes], gaze into the middle distance. Light glitters off perfect strangers. Whirling around, focused on the shadows, in the gaps between heads and long ears... was that her? No... it couldn’t be. No... nothing. Paranoia.
		
		Why are you still panting? Your heart refuses to settle. What made you come here in the first place? You should leave. An air of unease settles on you.
		
		You get scarce.
		"]))),[function(){return e}]

textify(K1||(K1=(0,Ce.Z)(["
		Dhaal’s acid-washed Gyre whistles all around. Think of the grittiest, dirtiest, slummiest hellhole in yesteryear’s harshest dystopias... you might start to feel like a [pc.manWoman] toeing the edge, a rebel with nothing to live for, trying to do some good for [pc.himHer]self or others. Thought experiment aside, you’re glad to avoid any xenophobic harassment. In fact, you receive a compliment!
		
		But in hindsight, it was perhaps an attempt to lure you into a trap. There’s as many gangs lurking in the shadows as there are streetwalkers in the neon-lit bowels of the Gyre.
		
		One roundabout offers an unusual reprieve from the zaika. There’s no screaming for a few seconds. Everything goes to a quiet droning hum; hover-traffic swoops overhead, jockeying for positions. Something breathes in your [pc.ear]; gooseflesh pimples across your [pc.skin]. Your head instantly whips to the side and back. Nobody passed you. Nothing’s missing or out of place...
		
		You listen, intently... and nothing. When you exhale, the breathing in your ear starts up again, as though it knows how to avoid compromising itself. These streets are like an overworld dungeon: let your guard down and it will eat you alive. A neon signboard blossoms to life nearby, pushing back the darkness of the alleyways. Nothing.
		
		Nerves are woken by the rabbit-eared shadow cast by some approaching Rushers. It draws ominously towards you, as if an entirely separate entity from its host. Sophora immediately comes to mind. Are you being stalked? If you were, it couldn’t be her... could it? There are so many places to hide. So many places for her to leap out, jump down... take a shot.
		
		Surely, it’s nothing. You’re overreacting. Walk softly, and keep your [pc.weapon] close.
		"])))

But, I got to know her, and what she told me was... well,” you lean back and fold your arms. “Not something I want to repeat, and not in her stead. She came from a bad place, ended up homeless, and addicted to a lot of drugs.”

\n\nBringing her on board was, at first, a chance to keep someone you cared about close. As you learned more about her, though, you were glad to give her a roof over her head, a place to sleep, and a real friend.

\n\n“I won’t pry, [pc.name],” Bianca sighs. “It’s unfortunate. Not anyone would help someone out like that. Too many times I’ve seen morality espoused and the speaker flaking away when the time came to stick through the worst of something. I hope she’ll be able to recover, in time.”
\n\nYou nod, not about to let slip you may have worsened her condition.
\n\nYou nod. There’s always hope.

\n\n[gooarmor.name] chimes in before you can say anything. “I’m on [pc.name]’s crew!” The nanobot gel makes herself known, "+(pc.inventory.armor.is(ke.S)?"head peeking up around yours":"slithering out of your pack")+". “I’m [gooarmor.name], and [pc.name]’s been the bestest friend ever! We go on so many adventures and we’re always having fun and I’m learning so much!”

\n\nR-Right. You explain to the bemused doctor how you met [gooarmor.name]. After helping the original crew of Novahome, she had appeared, and has been a helpful presence on your journey. She functions as an adaptable suit of armor and a companion all in one, bound together by insatiable curiosity and quick thinking. To be honest, you can’t wait to see what else she learns on your journey.
\n\n“Interesting...” Bianca waves to the bubbly goo-girl. “I admit, I don’t know what to say about that, but I’m glad you’ve met someone else you can trust.”
\n\nThat’s an understatement when it comes to [gooarmor.name]...

\n\n“Something peculiar happened to me, but I’m getting used to that. [gooarmor.name] is a gray-goo girl that joined me some time ago. She’s... it’s hard to describe. She’s a techno-gel, by all accounts, but she’s so excitable, so hyper. She loves helping me, loves learning new things, and despite how childlike she behaves, she’s quick to pick up on new things. She has too many functions to list out, but she’s... protective, I’ll say.”
\n\nYou do briefly talk about what she’s capable of, ending your recitation with a remark about how you can’t wait to see what else she learns on your journey.
\n\nBianca’s smile can be seen from orbit. “Sounds like when humanity encountered Galotians for the first time. Be careful what you expose her to, hm?”
\n\nYou’ll try.

\n\nTraveling the frontier is dangerous enough. That’s why you hired a kaithrit merc worth her credits. <i>“When I was on Tavros I "+(void 0===flags.RAMIS_TIMES_BENDER?"met":"befriended")+" a kaithrit woman named Ramis, and learned
through copious amounts of boozings

that she’s a no-frills mercenary behind her jacked exterior. My cousin’s got enough advantages, so I felt it was time to even it just a little. Makes me feel a lot safer, and it’s good to have a training partner.”

\n\n“It is. I considered hiring a bodyguard, but my drone’s been adequate enough.” Bianca nods thoughtfully. “And, to be honest, I’ve encountered a lot less trouble than I expected. That can always change, but, certainly, if you’re going to be traveling to dangerous places, you can never pay for enough protection.”
\n\nAin’t that the truth.

\n\nMany enemies you faced on Zhèng Shi, but you found friends among them. “While traversing Zhèng Shi, I became familiar with a dzaan woman named Ardia. She was abducted on one of the pirate’s many slave runs. I managed to save her from a sordid fate, and I’ve taken her on board for as long as she’d like.”
\n\n“But...” Bianca glare at you sternly, “is she an alpha or a beta?”

\n\nArdia is your alpha, and you are her beta. You are the first of many moons that will orbit her. Your slavish adoration for the dzaan who’s dominated your mind would continue if you were not so empathic to Bianca’s increasingly perturbed expression. When the fantasies of nuzzling Ardia’s infinite ballsack fade you rapidly shake your head and raise your hands in a calming gesture, very aware of how close you came to looking like a mindbroken [pc.manWoman].

\n\nWhat tumbles out of your mouth next is reassurance. Reassurance that you are always going to be [pc.name] Steele and will always have your identity. Ardia, and her biology, are things you accepted. You wanted a deeper relationship with her, and weren’t afraid of the consequences. In all honesty, while you are under the effects of her intoxicating sperm, you have full control. You are still you, and to your relief, Bianca accepts that answer.

\n\n“I’m happy for you, then, [pc.name].” Her voice comes out in a terse timbre that gradually softens. “It’s not my place to judge... but it still hurts me to see... what you were doing. Nothing terrifies me more than a person so full of life losing everything; their identity, their personality, their intelligence, all of it, just to be reduced to a-a-a... a toy. Please, promise me you’ll hold on to yourself no matter what. I care about you, and I want the best for you. I just hope that ‘the best’ isn’t giving yourself up.”
\n\nIt’s not. Ardia may be the best thing ever to you right now, but she’s just an inseparable part of the born beta: [pc.name] Steele.

\n\nShe’s your alpha. You couldn’t stop yourself from mouthing those damning words, and Bianca half-recoils. You raise your hands in desperation, reassuring vocabulary spilling forth. You aren’t her slave, you aren’t a mindless puppet. No, you have a real relationship with Ardia, one that’s deepened to a point you can taste her in your dreams. For Bianca’s sake, you don’t speak that last part. Nor any of the other lurid fantasies coming to mind.

\n\n“[pc.name], you’re addicted to her,” Bianca says flatly, snapping her finger in front of your eyes. “But you’re not at some point of no return. Even dzaan biology has its limits. I don’t begrudge you your choice in deepening your relationship with someone like her, but the effect it can have on you scares me. I hope you understand fully what may happen should you continue, and I hope that if it does happen... you don’t lose yourself...”
\n\nThe only thing you intend to lose yourself to is the world of pleasure that comes from being a beta. What Bianca hears, though, is your resolve to retain your identity.

\n\nAn alpha, certainly. Before Bianca gets into a tizzy, you "+(void 0!==flags.ARDIA_WHACKED_OFF_AGROSH?"explain that ingesting her cum was necessary, a life or death moment on the station. ":"explain that while your thoughts do occasionally wander for your alph- crew member’s companionship, you’re not a simpering puppet to narcotic sperm.")

\n\n“[pc.name]...” The foxy doctor looks mildly harmed by the revelation. “There are suppressants-” She stops herself, hand over her mouth. “No, that’s not for me to say. I just... I just don’t want to see you lose yourself. Dzaan biology is uniquely attuned to their race and specific culture. If you go too far you may lose yourself, and that frightens me to the core. I implore you to be careful. Please.”
\n\nWhatever happens, you don’t plan on losing your identity, regardless of how deep your alpha-beta relationship goes.
\n\nAn alpha, you explain, knowing that the doctor is worried about your mental capacity. You haven’t ingested any of her narcotic sperm. By your account, Ardia’s been forthcoming and respectful.

\n\n“I see, I see...” Bianca sighs happily. “Their biology is... while I understand it, it makes me uncomfortable. It’s part of their race’s culture and their entire way of life, but what it can do to others, especially when misapplied... it frightens me. Please exercise all due caution when you are with her, okay? I know this sounds rude and uncouth, but there’s a very real consequence to bedding an alpha dzaan, and while many say they are prepared for it, they typically aren’t.”
\n\nYou’ll be fine.

\n\nCommandeering the Sidewinder from the pirates on Zhèng Shi may just have been your boldest move on the Rush. Doing so came with an extra benefit: the gynoid assistant Olympia. “I’ve told you about Zhèng Shi, and about the ship they were building there. I stole that ship,” you say, with no small amount of pride, “and I gained an ally doing so. The scientist responsible for its construction and design, a gryvain-styled android named Olympia, joined the crew and my company as well. In a way, she was a slave to her creator, but now she’s keeping things running on my quest through the stars.”
\n\n“Goodness,” Bianca laughs. “I’d like to meet her someday. I wonder how deeper her knowledge runs? Still, I bet it’s nice to have friends like that on your trip!”
\n\nIt is, and now your trip just got a whole lot more intense!

\n\nMost of the Jumpers on Zhèng Shi were dopey fuckbunnies, there’s no two ways about it. Their leader is a dangerous and unpredictable bitch who runs the biggest crew in the place. But of all those addictive, sweaty, musky rabbits, you did meet one unique individual among them. Or, rather, she met you.
\n\n“Would you believe me if I told you I’ve hired on a former pirate to my crew?” you ask Bianca.
\n\nShe folds her arms, saying nothing. Her raised eyebrow does the talking.

\n\n<i>“Leila Harper, a laquine. She was part of one of the gangs there. She offered me assistance on my way through the station, and as I got to know her, the story she told had convinced me she was eager to reform. I decided to give her passage out of the station
, hiring her into my company as well so that she can start her life over
. She’s looking for her sister, Reina, who went missing a while back, so while traveling with me, she has the best chance of finding her... and I have a loyal companion.”

\n\n“Leila Harper... I’m not familiar with the name, but I guess that’s no surprise. If I hear anything about her sister, I’ll let you know. In the meantime... no, nevermind. I can see it in your eye,” Bianca smirks, “you trust Leila, and I trust your judgment.”
\n\n“Zhèng Shi isn’t just home to several dangerous crews of pirates, but it’s home to freelancers, mercenaries... and even a bustling casino,” you start.
\n\nBianca tilts her head with a silly grin. “I can’t imagine the odds are very good for anyone but the house in such a den of iniquity.”
\n\n“No,” you say, “but beating those odds is how I gave someone a new lease on life.”
\n\nThe question mark etched on Bianca’s mien is as apparent as the shine behind her glasses.
\n\n<i>“A blackjack dealer; a kaithrit named Roo. I’d played her so many times, won so many times, that the casino owner fired her and offered her to me. I bought her contract
and freed her
. For the time being, she flies with me. I think she’s a lot younger than you,” you wink playfully, “but she has that same warmth and... adoration that you have.”

\n\nRoo faced a life of servitude, obedience, and submission, and did it all with optimism, grace, and a winning smile. Her prediction of living her life solely as an unfree automaton was shattered when you came to her and won her freedom. Bianca’s ears swivel and she nods appreciatively.
\n\n“I’m happy to hear about that, [pc.name]! Please, introduce me to her someday. For you to speak so highly of her, I would love the opportunity.”
\n\nMaybe someday.

Bianca’s sly grin nudges at you before her voice, which has already sanitized itself of gloom. “So, what did you decide for your career in your earlier life, [pc.name]? I would hope your father offered you some reasonable starting points.”

\n\n“He did,” you say flatly. “There were a few suggestions, but I decided to forge myself as a mercenary. "+(flags.PC_UPBRINGING===GLOBAL.UPBRINGING_ATHLETIC?"I always loved to exercise myself and it was a natural choice to apply myself at a military academy.":"I entered a military academy and honed myself physically.")+"”

\n\nMost of your education there is a bit of a blur, but you do go into detail about the things you learned. How to pilot vehicles like tanks and gunships. How you were also beaten into the ground harder and harder, fortifying yourself on dad’s dime until the time came you were putting the instructor down. For how hard the days were, you don’t regret them at all.

\n\nYou gained the kind of survival skills necessary to make it out on the Rush, to look after yourself. Once or twice you served as a bodyguard, and many more times you worked under men and women with serious and damn near infallible records.

\n\nFrom a father, there could be no better gift or preparation. You handle weapons and armaments like a pro, and given enough time, you think you’ll surpass some of the legends in the field. Until then, though, it’s not a choice you’ve come to regret.

\n\n“I’m glad to hear it, [pc.name]!” Bianca jovially exclaims, patting your shoulder. “Isn’t that the best kind of feeling? To be able to rise above your station, show everyone what you learned? I found it exhilarating myself, hee hee... my instructor almost didn’t want to put hands on me. He said I was so frail!”

\n\nThe conversation continues in the midst of your dying laughter, until you’ve both swapped enough stories about the intricacies of self-defense. There’s only so many details you can share about workout routines, diets, how to hone one’s reflexes...

\n\n“You could say that,” you reply with a sly, widening grin. “Dear old dad gave me a few unique suggestions but there’s one I took to like I was born for it. "+(flags.PC_UPBRINGING===GLOBAL.UPBRINGING_AUSTERE?"I learned to earn everything in life from very early on and I was used to moving around from house to house, so it’s no surprise I guess that I ended up learning the traits of":"I learned to get used to moving around and enjoying every bit of rest I could, since I chose to become")+" a... smuggler. It might sound bad, but I inspected all cargo and never ran dangerous goods, though some were definitely illegal to own or to use. Or both.”

\n\n“Is that so, [pc.name]? Imagine the headlines, hee hee. The [pc.son] of Steele Tech’s founder, the inheritor of the company, little more than a black market courier! Oh, you must have some tales!”

\n\nYou have one, in fact. Cutting your teeth as a quick-witted, agile, ‘doesn’t stay in one place too long’ kind of [pc.guyGirl] wasn’t as routine as being a merc or mentally oriented as being a geek. Relying on gut instinct was the name of the game. Being aware, alert, street smart, space smart... all sorts of things. It’s not for the faint of heart, and not for poor decision makers.

\n\nBut you’re digressing: you ran with a small time gang once, and they had plans for the entire system. Long story short, you explain to the strangely young and enraptured looking Bianca how you rolled with them, how you learned the tricks of your trade... then made out like a bandit when they got too big for their britches. When they started getting into more and more dangerous games, you got out, putting their leadership in jail and making off with most of the earnings from a recent job.
\n\n“Oh, it sounds like such an adventure! Maybe someday I’ll get to see you being cool for myself?”
\n\nReally? ‘Cool’? What’s gotten into her?

\n\n“B-But, [pc.name]... I don’t know... I just really like confident people! Doctors are trained to handle everything professionally, to be impartial, to be cool under fire. There are people who are so naturally suited to the concept like you, and I... I really admire that! A brain that thinks of every angle, and thinks further than all the others! But, just tell me one thing... did you ever get arrested?”
\n\nHah. No.
\n\n“Thought not,” she grins, “but, humor me! I want to hear so much more.”

\n\nCertainly. You came into contact with just about every race there is out there, a handler of every species. It wasn’t actually that dangerous as it turns out, just exhausting. Being in and out of so many apartments, running for dead drops, eluding a mall cop or two... like a glorified teenage adventure in the end. Once you run out of thrills to share, Bianca kisses you on the cheek.
\n\nYep. You really didn’t expect her to be so enamored with your lifestyle.

\n\n“That’s correct,” you reply, brimming with confidence. “Dad gave me a few, err, unique suggestions. "+(flags.PC_UPBRINGING===GLOBAL.UPBRINGING_BOOKWORM?"I loved reading about technology, I loved keeping up with it all, and I loved deconstructing and reconstructing it. I devoured as many books as I could on engineering and computer programming too, taking to the career of a Technical Specialist like it was made for me.":"I loved technology, and I loved working with the latest advancements, which is why I trained diligently as a Technical Specialist.")+"”

\n\n“I’m the same way about books!” Bianca chimes. “Technical abilities were never my passion, but I use a drone for protection when I work in these dangerous areas -- but you know that. Still, I minored in technical engineering and have treated it as sort of a light hobby ever since.”

\n\nVictor had you enroll in one of the most expensive (and reputable) universities in the U.G.C. There you learned the fundamentals of engineering, computer science... you programmed your own basic V.I., and learned how to perform maintenance on ships, vehicles, weapons...

\n\nThe list does indeed go on and on, and you could nerd out for as many hours as you’ve left to live! The one thing you were most proud of though is how you learned to void every warranty that came within a fifty-mile radius of you and improve your gear with modifications suited to whatever task was at hand.
\n\n“A regular tinkerer, huh?” Bianca props her face up prettily. “Your title means so much, but it also sounds so vague... what else can you tell me? Please! I’d love to hear more!”

\n\nThere’s a lot to tell, but most of it is boring technical stuff that would siphon the life right out of this conversation. You instead steer it towards a common topic: drones. It’s a subject you picked right up on, knowing you’d need the extra protection out on the frontier, and just about anywhere really. Being able to prop up the holes in your fighting style with a robotic companion equipped for a variety of means has kept you safe, and you’ve no doubt it’ll continue to pay off.

\n\nPausing for a moment, you add-on that you’ve always had an affinity for energy weapons too. No recoil, rechargeable batteries... now there’s a subject you could spend far too long on. Rather than that, you banter back and forth about firmware and programming, trading insights on what makes a ‘bot put in an ounce more work than usual.
\n\nChecking your chrono after you both rest your lungs, you’re not sure if you should be shocked that two hours passed!
